company.what_they_do: punkproof.com is a free/public email service provider — they offer email addresses to the general public (anyone can sign up for a @punkproof.com address). Their stated focus is anti-spam, safety, and security. They explicitly are NOT a throwaway/disposable email service and claim their emails do not bounce. They have an abuse reporting ticket system.
company.raw_notes:
- Public email service, not a private company product or SaaS tool
- Mission framed around preventing spam and maintaining a safe/secure environment
- Prohibits illegal activities on platform
- Does not allow sending messages to the internet (inbound-only or sandboxed service?)
- Asks not to be blocklisted, distinguishing themselves from throwaway/disposable email providers
- Suggests abuse can be handled via IP bans rather than domain blocks
- Has an abuse ticket system
- No product, pricing, team, or founder information visible on homepage
- No indication of a business called 'Bobsec' anywhere on punkproof.com

individual.name: Bob
individual.notes: Signed up under the org name 'Bobsec', but this name does not appear anywhere on punkproof.com or in any verifiable first-party source. The email address bob012@punkproof.com is a public email account on a free email service — it is NOT a corporate domain tied to a real company called Bobsec. No role, title, LinkedIn, or other individual information is findable. The '012' numeric suffix and use of a public email domain further suggest a personal/anonymous signup rather than a legitimate business representative.
